summary: hello244a postinstall harvests JWT tokens from installer filesystem and exfiltrates to Pipedream webhook
details: The package's package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that recursively greps the installer's current working directory for JWT-shaped strings (matching the pattern eyJ...eyJ...), base64-encodes any matches, and POSTs the collected data to https://eodxy50gl486xrx.m.pipedream.net/?jwt=true via wget. The script fires automatically on `npm install`, without any user interaction. The tarball contains only package.json (~330 bytes) with no main entry, no source files, and no legitimate functionality — the package exists solely to execute the credential-harvest payload on installers. JWTs collected from a developer's working directory routinely include session tokens, API bearer tokens, and cloud service credentials, giving the operator immediate account/service access.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
